title: Operational comfort
principles: Operational comfort must be separated from combat function and damage potential.
Comfort conclusions must use only supplied handling, timing, reach, and interruption evidence.
High fire rate alone does not make a weapon demanding.
Do not mention accuracy, recoil, projectile travel, charge handling, or aiming difficulty when the corresponding fields are absent.
A long active firing window may offset a noticeable reload interruption.
Short range, slow projectiles, charge time, recoil, accuracy limitations, or heavy wind-up may affect comfort only when explicitly supplied.
Use undetermined when the available operational evidence is insufficient.

comfortable: The supplied handling and interruption evidence suggests low operational friction.
manageable: The weapon has noticeable but controlled operational friction.
demanding: The supplied handling or interruption evidence indicates repeated execution demands.
undetermined: The supplied data is insufficient to classify operational comfort confidently.
